payload.text:
Project Overview
Thalam is a cultural intelligence platform that visualizes living traditions as interconnected, dynamic systems. The app operates as a single-view exploration interface (not a multi-panel dashboard) where the Pulse Map dominates by default. Secondary modes (DNA Explorer, Trend Harmonics) are accessed as full-screen transitions. The Guru AI assistant is strictly opt-in and only appears on user invocation.
Design Principle Alignment

Canvas-Dominant: Full-screen Pulse Map as default state
Progressive Disclosure: Panels only appear when invoked
Atmospheric Over Navigational: Soft overlays, not traditional map UI
Cultural Respect: Scholarly AI, no sensationalism
One Interaction Per Screen: No persistent split-screen layouts

Architecture & Tech Stack

Framework: Next.js (App Router)
UI Components: shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS
Map Visualization: Leaflet.js + OpenStreetMap base layer
Advanced Overlays: Deck.gl integration (soft heatmaps, clustering)
AI Service: Google Gemini API (server-side only, secure)
Data Abstraction: Custom lib/data-provider.ts (enables future API migration)
State Management: React hooks with Context API
Graph Visualization: vis.js for Cultural DNA Explorer
Charts: Recharts for Trend Harmonics

Data Structure & Abstraction Layer
Critical: Data Provider Abstraction
File: lib/data-provider.ts
All UI components must consume cultural data through this abstraction layer, NOT directly from JSON files. This ensures:

JSON can be replaced by API without UI rewrites
Future ingestion pipeline integration is seamless
Data transformation logic is centralized

Provider Interface:
- getCulturalEvents(filters?: any)
- getEventById(id: string)
- getTraditions()
- getTraditionById(id: string)
- getRelationships(entityId: string)
- searchByVibe(query: string)

Data Storage

public/data/cultural-events.json - Source data file
Provider abstracts access patterns from components
Future: Can switch to API endpoint in provider without touching UI

Gemini Integration (Server-Side Secure)
CRITICAL SECURITY REQUIREMENT

Remove: NEXT_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY
API Key Location: .env.local (server-side only)
Access Pattern: All Gemini calls go through API route
API Route: app/api/guru-insight.ts (handles streaming)
Frontend: Never directly calls Gemini API

Flow:

User invokes Guru (floating orb)
Frontend sends query to /api/guru-insight
API route uses server-side env key to call Gemini
Stream response back to frontend
Frontend displays streamed content as paragraph narration

MVP Implementation Plan
PRIORITY 1: Foundation Layer (Must Complete First)
Phase 1A: Data Abstraction & JSON Structure


Create Data Provider (lib/data-provider.ts)

Abstraction interface for all data access
Load from JSON initially (easily replaced by API)
Export all data types and interfaces



Create Cultural Events Dataset (public/data/cultural-events.json)

Sample Kerala cultural events with:

id, name, location (lat/long)
tradition/ritual type
description, significance
related_traditions (array of IDs)
seasonal_markers, tags
emotional_resonance


Minimum 15-20 diverse events for visualization



TypeScript Types (lib/types.ts)

CulturalEvent, Tradition, Relationship interfaces
Ensure type safety across components



Phase 1B: Pulse Map (Full-Screen Default)


Main App Shell (app/page.tsx)

Render full-screen Pulse Map by default
State management for:

Selected event (none by default)
Active visualization mode (Pulse | DNA Explorer | Trend Harmonics)
Guru panel open/closed (false by default)


No persistent multi-panel layout



Pulse Map Component (components/pulse-map.tsx)

Leaflet map with OpenStreetMap base layer
Visualization Approach:

Soft heatmap overlays (Deck.gl GeoJsonLayer with transparency)
Organic clustering (Deck.gl clustering)
Subtle pulse animations (CSS + Framer Motion)
Remove: Traditional map markers/pins


Interactions:

Hover on cluster: Whisper effect (opacity change)
Click on cluster/heatmap area: Trigger detail
Zoom: Progressive detail reveal


Deck.gl Layer Strategy:

Keep overlay abstraction clean (not tightly coupled to Leaflet DOM)
Enable future migration to pure WebGL or MapLibre


Controls:

Seasonal time slider (bottom center, minimal styling)
Layer toggle icons (floating, not docked to sidebar)
No persistent legend





Phase 1C: Cultural Insight Sheet (Overlay Only)

Insight Sheet Component (components/cultural-insight-sheet.tsx)

Appears only after user selects a cultural event from map
Bottom slide-up overlay (semi-transparent glass effect)
Content Structure:

Ritual/event name and location
Brief cultural description (max 100 words)
"Cultural DNA Similarity" bar (visual indicator of connections)
"Related Traditions" (horizontal scrolling flow)
"Why This Matters" (contextual snippet)


Constraints:

Max 2 scroll lengths (no fatigue)
Map remains visible behind (glass effect)
Close button or swipe-down to dismiss


Consumes data from lib/data-provider.ts



PRIORITY 2: Advanced Features
Phase 2A: Guru AI Assistant (Opt-In, Non-Intrusive)


Floating Orb Component (components/guru-orb.tsx)

Small floating button (bottom-right or right edge)
Always visible but dormant
Click to invoke (never auto-opens)



Guru Panel (components/guru-panel.tsx)

Expands from orb into side panel (smooth animation)
Behavior Rules:

Only appears on invocation
Paragraph narration (NOT chat bubbles)
Responses stream line-by-line
Key phrases subtly glow
"Why" button under responses for follow-up
Can reference selected event on map for context


Cultural Output Rules (from AI Behavior doc):

All responses must reference source data
Distinguish between fact, interpretation, inference
Scholarly tone, never sensational
Respect spiritual contexts
Output categories: Sourced Fact | Scholarly Interpretation | Model Inference





API Integration (app/api/guru-insight.ts)

Receives query + optional context (selected event)
Calls Gemini API with server-side key
System prompt:

Role: Scholarly guide to cultural traditions
Never hallucinate unsourced facts
Label uncertainty explicitly
Respectful, non-exoticizing language


Streams response back to frontend
Handles error cases gracefully



Phase 2B: Cultural DNA Explorer (Full-Screen Mode)


DNA Explorer Component (components/cultural-dna-explorer.tsx)

Transition to full-screen visualization (not side-by-side)
Visualization:

Center ritual anchors the space
Related rituals orbit with similarity glow
Neural-style node visualization (vis.js)
Lines show relationships (influenced-by, related-to, derives-from)


Interactions:

Drag to rearrange nodes
Click node to expand similarity radius
"Morph-to-map" animation when selecting event (returns to Pulse Map with selection)


Aesthetic: Feels like Spotify Wrapped × Museum Lab
Uses data from provider for relationships



Navigation:

Tab or menu to switch between Pulse, DNA Explorer, Harmonics
DNA Explorer is accessible from Insight Sheet or main nav



PRIORITY 3: Trend Harmonics (Professional Mode)
Phase 3A: Trend Analytics Dashboard

Trend Harmonics Component (components/trend-harmonics.tsx)

Full-screen mode (accessed via nav)
Design Constraint: NOT corporate dashboard aesthetic

Glassmorphism panels (not card-based)
Generous white space
Calm, institutional tone
Minimal color, high clarity


Key Visualizations:

At-risk traditions (list with preservation score)
Engagement decay curves (seasonal patterns)
Regional distribution map (simplified)
Tradition evolution timeline (subtle chart)


Content Philosophy:

Trade poetry for responsibility
Clear data labeling
No KPI-style metrics
Avoid chart density





Component Architecture
/app
  /api
    /guru-insight.ts (Server-side Gemini integration)
  layout.tsx (App wrapper, global state)
  page.tsx (Main entry point, Pulse Map default)

/components
  pulse-map.tsx (Leaflet + Deck.gl map)
  cultural-insight-sheet.tsx (Event detail overlay)
  guru-orb.tsx (Floating AI button)
  guru-panel.tsx (AI response display)
  cultural-dna-explorer.tsx (Network graph)
  trend-harmonics.tsx (Analytics dashboard)
  nav-switcher.tsx (Mode tabs: Pulse | DNA | Harmonics)

/lib
  data-provider.ts (CRITICAL: Data abstraction layer)
  types.ts (TypeScript interfaces)
  gemini-prompt.ts (System prompt for Guru)
  data-transform.ts (Helpers for graph/chart data)

/public/data
  cultural-events.json (Source dataset)

Key Implementation Details
Deck.gl + Leaflet Pattern (Avoid Lock-In)

Deck.gl layers are rendered as overlay on Leaflet map
Abstraction: components/deck-layer-manager.tsx handles all Deck.gl logic
Leaflet remains flexible for future replacement
If migrating to pure WebGL or MapLibre, only overlay manager changes

Data Provider Pattern (Abstraction Example)
// Components never do this:
import culturalData from '@/public/data/cultural-events.json'

// Components always do this:
import { useDataProvider } from '@/lib/data-provider'
const { events } = useDataProvider()
Guru Streaming Response

Use app/api/guru-insight.ts with streaming response
Frontend reads stream and renders line-by-line
No speech bubbles (paragraph narration only)
Context: Can optionally pass selected event data to API

Design Specifications (From Design Document)
Color Palette

Deep charcoal black (primary canvas)
Indigo shadows (depth)
Ritual amber (warm glow for UI accents)
Vermilion red (energy states)
Temple gold (heritage markers)
Muted cyan (AI signals only)
Rule: No pure white, no bright neon

Typography

Modern humanist sans-serif (body, clarity)
Soft serif/classical tone (section titles only)
Large breathing headlines
Generous line-height
Minimal text density

Motion & Animation

Nothing snaps, everything flows
Heatmaps pulse subtly (breathing rhythm)
Panels slide, never pop
Hover states glow, not highlight
AI responses fade in line-by-line
Duration: 300–600ms, ease-in-out only

Accessibility

WCAG AA+ color contrast
Motion-reduced mode support
ARIA labels on interactive elements
Keyboard navigation for all controls
Respectful, non-exoticizing language

Libraries to Install

leaflet + react-leaflet (mapping)
@deck.gl/react + @deck.gl/geo-layers (advanced overlays)
vis-network (graph visualization)
recharts (charts)
@google/generative-ai (Gemini SDK for server-side use)
framer-motion (animations)
zustand or context API (state management)

Gemini Integration Checklist

✅ API key in .env.local (server-side only)
✅ No NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix on Gemini key
✅ /api/guru-insight.ts handles all Gemini calls
✅ Frontend never directly calls Gemini
✅ Streaming response implementation
✅ System prompt aligned with AI Behavior guidelines
✅ Error handling for API failures

Implementation Order (Strict Sequence)


Setup Phase

Add libraries to package.json
Create .env.local with Gemini key (server-side)



Data Phase (CRITICAL FIRST)

Create lib/data-provider.ts abstraction
Create lib/types.ts interfaces
Populate public/data/cultural-events.json



Pulse Map Phase

Build app/page.tsx main layout
Implement components/pulse-map.tsx
Add Deck.gl heatmap overlay
Test data loading via provider



Insight Sheet Phase

Implement components/cultural-insight-sheet.tsx
Wire up click-to-select interaction on map
Test slide-up animation



Guru AI Phase

Create /api/guru-insight.ts endpoint
Implement components/guru-orb.tsx
Implement components/guru-panel.tsx
Test streaming responses



DNA Explorer Phase

Implement components/cultural-dna-explorer.tsx
Create mode switcher navigation
Test graph data transformation



Trend Harmonics Phase

Implement components/trend-harmonics.tsx
Build visualization panels
Wire up navigation



Polish & Refinement

Animations (Framer Motion)
Responsive design
Accessibility audit
Performance optimization



Success Criteria
✅ Pulse Map renders full-screen by default with atmospheric heatmap visualization
✅ Clicking on map area opens Insight Sheet as bottom overlay
✅ Guru orb appears but never auto-opens; only invokes on click
✅ Guru responses stream as paragraph narration, not chat bubbles
✅ DNA Explorer is separate full-screen mode, accessible via nav
✅ Trend Harmonics displays calm, professional insights (no corporate dashboard feel)
✅ All data access goes through lib/data-provider.ts
✅ Gemini API key never exposed to frontend
✅ Deck.gl overlay abstracted for future migration flexibility
✅ App feels atmospheric and intentional, not tool-like
Critical Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

No 4-panel dashboard layout
No auto-opening Guru panel
No persistent side panels
No custom map marker pins
No NEXT_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY
No tight coupling between Deck.gl and Leaflet
No component direct-importing from JSON files
No speech bubbles in AI responses
Trend Harmonics must feel institutional, not corporate

payload.text: add gemini api for actively search for new cultures and making cultural dna explorer

**Developing Cultural Explorer**

I'm focusing on integrating the Gemini API to dynamically seek new cultural information, rather than relying on the hardcoded data in `lib/`. My goal is to build a robust, actively-updated "cultural DNA explorer." I'm prioritizing efficient API calls and effective data parsing to create an engaging experience for the user.


**Expanding Cultural Knowledge**

I'm focused on integrating a dynamic search function using the Gemini API to find new cultural events and traditions. The plan is to create a robust and updated "Cultural DNA Explorer" by leveraging the API to build the relationships and nodes instead of relying on static mock data. I'm prioritizing the addition of the new function `searchCulturalTraditions(query: string)` within `services/guru.ts` and will use `responseSchema` to guarantee Gemini returns valid JSON. This will provide structured data to the Cultural DNA Explorer.


**Implementing Search & Expansion**

I'm now integrating a search bar within `cultural-dna-explorer.tsx` to call the Gemini service when the user wants to expand a node. This will dynamically update the `vis-network` graph with new nodes and edges returned, which will be locally managed in the component. I'm focusing on ensuring data types from the service function are correctly exported, and for now, the map won't include a search function.


**Adding Discover Functionality**

I'm currently working to add a "Discover" functionality. I'm focused on creating a `discoverCulturalConnections` function within `services/guru.ts` that will query Gemini. I'm planning to use the `responseSchema` to parse the resulting JSON. In `cultural-dna-explorer.tsx`, I intend to integrate an input field for search and create two functions: `handleSearch()` to fetch results from the discover function, and `handleExpandNode()` to expand a particular node. The node and edge data will then be merged into the existing graph.


**Refining Data Handling**

I'm now thinking about how to handle the data I'm getting from Gemini. Though a "Dynamic Data Provider" in `lib/data-provider.ts` would be nice for better organization, it seems like managing the data locally within the component is enough for now. The focus is on the API call structure with the `responseSchema`, specifically the JSON format for nodes and edges.


**Adapting Node Data Format**

I'm now focusing on transforming the Gemini output to be compatible with `vis-network`. Specifically, I need to ensure the data structure returned fits `vis-network`'s requirements. This means adjusting the node and edge formats within `cultural-dna-explorer.tsx`. I will also work to add the `discoverConnections` function in `services/guru.ts` and add the required UI in `cultural-dna-explorer.tsx`. I will verify that the node and edge data can be merged correctly with the existing graph.


**Defining Content Generation**

export const useDataProvider = () => {
  const getCulturalEvents = (filter?: string) => {
    if (!filter) return CULTURAL_EVENTS;
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => e.type === filter);
  };

  const getEventById = (id: string) => {
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.find(e => e.id === id);
  };

  const getRelatedEvents = (id: string) => {
    const event = getEventById(id);
    if (!event) return [];
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => event.related_traditions.includes(e.id));
  };

  return {
    events: CULTURAL_EVENTS,
    getCulturalEvents,
    getEventById,
    getRelatedEvents
  };
};

export const useDataProvider = () => {
  const getCulturalEvents = (filter?: string) => {
    if (!filter) return CULTURAL_EVENTS;
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => e.type === filter);
  };

  const getEventById = (id: string) => {
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.find(e => e.id === id);
  };

  const getRelatedEvents = (id: string) => {
    const event = getEventById(id);
    if (!event) return [];
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => event.related_traditions.includes(e.id));
  };

  return {
    events: CULTURAL_EVENTS,
    getCulturalEvents,
    getEventById,
    getRelatedEvents
  };
};

export const useDataProvider = () => {
  const getCulturalEvents = (filter?: string) => {
    if (!filter) return CULTURAL_EVENTS;
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => e.type === filter);
  };

  const getEventById = (id: string) => {
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.find(e => e.id === id);
  };

  const getRelatedEvents = (id: string) => {
    const event = getEventById(id);
    if (!event) return [];
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => event.related_traditions.includes(e.id));
  };

  return {
    events: CULTURAL_EVENTS,
    getCulturalEvents,
    getEventById,
    getRelatedEvents
  };
};

    emotional_resonance: 'high',
    preservation_score: 90,
    dna_relationships: []
  }
];

export const useDataProvider = () => {
  const getCulturalEvents = (filter?: string) => {
    if (!filter) return CULTURAL_EVENTS;
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => e.type === filter);
  };

  const getEventById = (id: string) => {
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.find(e => e.id === id);
  };

  const getRelatedEvents = (id: string) => {
    const event = getEventById(id);
    if (!event) return [];
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => event.related_traditions.includes(e.id));
  };

  return {
    events: CULTURAL_EVENTS,
    getCulturalEvents,
    getEventById,
    getRelatedEvents
  };
};

export const useDataProvider = () => {
  const getCulturalEvents = (filter?: string) => {
    if (!filter) return CULTURAL_EVENTS;
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => e.type === filter);
  };

  const getEventById = (id: string) => {
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.find(e => e.id === id);
  };

  const getRelatedEvents = (id: string) => {
    const event = getEventById(id);
    if (!event) return [];
    return CULTURAL_EVENTS.filter(e => event.related_traditions.includes(e.id));
  };

  return {
    events: CULTURAL_EVENTS,
    getCulturalEvents,
    getEventById,
    getRelatedEvents
  };
};
